rows.measured_basis: PMU atlas + FMI-1/FMI-2 + decode integration tax ledger
rows.remaining_question: Can tinygrad preserve low-VGPR, high-grid MMVQ behavior and activation-format reuse inside the model?
rows.first_gate: B2 runtime/cache identity is closed; renderer/scheduler or source/artifact import only
rows.correctness_quality_gate: byte-identical unless q8 route is used; q8 requires dNLL gate
rows.amdahl_or_potential: 44->54% over 85% weight-GEMV bucket = ~1.187x decode; full 76% transfer = ~1.557x theoretical
refutations: another standalone GEMV kernel is not the issue
stage2 reduction alone is too small
existing Q4K/Q6K env knobs fail FMI-4 B1
dot4 alone is not a primitive
rows.next_action: No bounded diagnostic remains; choose project-level renderer/scheduler, source import, or the small q8 research flag.

rows.current_authority: B2 runtime/cache identity probe closes hidden wiring mismatch
rows.measured_basis: same-process in-model decode vs direct role calls: all high-share roles match program/launch identity
rows.remaining_question: none for runtime/cache identity; large decode movement now requires renderer/scheduler or artifact/import
rows.amdahl_or_potential: 0 from runtime/cache identity; it does not explain the 44->54% gap
refutations: do not retune env knobs; B1 already failed
rows.next_action: Do not pursue runtime/cache wiring fixes; choose renderer/scheduler or artifact/import for large decode movement.

rows.primitive_class: lossy q8 producer + Q4_K gate/up dot4 consumer
rows.current_authority: Q8_FFN_HANDWRITTEN route passes W==D + dNLL as research flag
rows.remaining_question: Policy only for research use; native ownership remains scheduler/codegen project-level
rows.first_gate: none for research; default requires policy decision and broader quality comfort
refutations: separate q8 pack loses
native current-UOp producer not expressible
native tinygrad ASM/COMGR variants miss artifact performance
rows.next_action: Keep default-off; use as oracle for native codegen, not main parity path.

rows.current_authority: llama.cpp has MMVQ source and build objects, but no Tensile-like standalone HCQ code-object family was found
rows.remaining_question: Only a source-contract import or renderer/scheduler project remains for large decode movement
rows.first_gate: closed for ready-artifact extraction; reopen only with an identified standalone code object or funded source-import project
rows.correctness_quality_gate: byte-exact for fp path or dNLL-gated for q8; HCQ no-HIP-runtime launch
rows.amdahl_or_potential: could target same 44->54%+ contract gap if an artifact preserves llama-like MMVQ behavior
refutations: HIP runtime bridge cannot coexist with tinygrad HCQ/KFD
single handwritten q8 route only covers gate/up
rows.next_action: Do not start TPE-style extraction; scope source-contract import only if accepting project-level work.

rows.current_authority: spec correctness exists, but T=5 verify is ~4.66x one pass and current speed model loses
rows.remaining_question: Only a broad batched-forward rewrite across Q4_K, Q6_K/lm_head, and attention could reopen it
rows.first_gate: verify <=1.5x one T==1 pass before route
rows.amdahl_or_potential: large if T-cheap verify exists; current path ~0.52x before overhead
refutations: single Q4_K verify kernel insufficient
component routes do not sum to required cut
rows.next_action: Do not build under current bounded primitive queue.

rows.current_authority: transpose-free Tensile is correct but 0.997x; tinygrad warmstart-WMMA matmul already matches Tensile-class speed in-model
rows.measured_basis: prefill transpose-free result: OFF 2675 tok/s, ON 2666 tok/s; gate/up kernel ~63 TFLOPS
rows.remaining_question: Which non-matmul pieces explain e2e-effective ~41 vs matmul-kernel ~63 TFLOPS?
rows.first_gate: prefill component atlas over attention, norms, residuals, activation transposes/casts, lm_head under warm pp authority
rows.correctness_quality_gate: byte-identical or dNLL <= 0.01 for lossy variants
rows.amdahl_or_potential: bounded by the ~35% non-matmul dilution; exact EV unknown until component atlas
refutations: Tensile fp16 kernel swap is e2e-neutral
transpose-free Tensile route is e2e-neutral
pure WMMA bounded knobs are not current bottleneck
rows.next_action: Run/map warm prefill non-matmul component attribution before more GEMM work.

rows.current_authority: HCQ extraction works and kernels are fast/correct, but as-built and transpose-free in-model routes are ~1.00x
rows.remaining_question: No speed reason to land for current pp512 target; keep as oracle/tooling asset
rows.first_gate: none; closed unless new non-matmul integration use appears

refutations: layout-tax removal did not improve e2e
WMMA in-model kernel already matches Tensile-class speed
rows.next_action: Do not pursue as performance route; use as backend-contract oracle only.

rows.current_authority: normal decode attention is small; ctx4096 shows KV streaming growth
rows.measured_basis: PMU atlas says normal decode is weight-GEMV-dominated, KV grows at long ctx
rows.remaining_question: At which ctx does KV/attention exceed the Amdahl threshold and what is the limiter?
rows.first_gate: ctx sweep; promote only if attention/KV share >=15% and plausible movement >=5% e2e
rows.correctness_quality_gate: exact for layout/engine, dNLL for KV quant
rows.amdahl_or_potential: target-dependent; not part of current pp512/base-decode benchmark
refutations: normal decode attention optimization is not a large residual
do not mix long-context rows into base decode
rows.next_action: Defer until long-context benchmark is accepted.
